title: Spinal in-situ bender
abstract: A spinal in-situ bender (10) is provided for bending an in-situ spinal rod (3). The in-situ bender (10) comprises: a shaft (11) defining a shaft axis, and a grip portion (23) at one end of the shaft (11). The in-situ bender (10) extends between a handling end (12) and an attachment end (13), which comprises a hook (16) with a passage for engaging with a spinal rod (3). The in-situ bender (10) further comprises a lever-based clamping mechanism (15) defining a locked state, in which the in-situ bender (10) is configured to clamp the spinal rod (3) within the hook (16), and an unlocked state, in which the in-situ bender (10) is configured to receive the spinal rod (3) within the hook (16) without clamping it. The clamping mechanism (15) is configured as a toggle clamp mechanism (14) or as a single-lever mechanism.
claims: Claims 1. A spinal in-situ bender (10) for bending an in-situ spinal rod (3), the in-situ bender (10) comprising: a shaft (11) defining a shaft axis (A1), and a grip portion (23) at one end of the shaft (11), the in-situ bender (10) extending between a handling end (12) and an attachment end (13), the attachment end comprising a hook (16) with a passage (17) for engaging with the spinal rod (3), the in-situ bender (10) further comprising a lever-based clamping mechanism (15) defining a locked state, in which the in-situ bender (10) is configured to clamp the spinal rod (3) within the hook (16), and an unlocked state, in which the in-situ bender (10) is configured to receive the spinal rod (3, 60) within the hook (16) without clamping it, wherein the clamping mechanism (15) is configured as a toggle clamp mechanism (14) or as a single-lever mechanism. 2. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to claim 1 , wherein the toggle clamp mechanism (14) comprises a first lever link (26) coupled to a body (32) of the in-situ bender (10) formed by the shaft (11) and the grip portion (23) by a first coupling and by a third coupling, and a second lever link (29) coupled to the body (32) by the first coupling and by a second coupling, and wherein in the locked state, the first, second and third couplings are aligned or substantially aligned. 3. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to claim 2, wherein the second lever link (29) is arranged at the handling end (12), and comprises a movement control feature (42) to reduce movability of the second lever link (29) in the grip portion (23), and wherein the movement control feature (42) is configured as a spring element (42) configured to be under tension in the grip portion (23). 4. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the single-lever mechanism is configured as a lever-guiding-face mechanism comprising a pin-in-slot coupling at the attachment end (13), a first guiding face (40) and a second guiding face (41) at the handling end (12), and arranged to directly or indirectly engage with each other and to move with respect to each other when the in-situ bender (10) transitions between the locked and unlocked states. 5. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to claim 4, wherein the first guiding face (40) is an elongated slot face, and the second guiding face (41 ) is an outer pin face, or vice versa. 6. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the clamping mechanism (14) comprises a locking element (24), the manipulation of which allows the in-situ bender (10) to reach the locked state, and a release element (25), the manipulation of which allows the in-situ bender (10) to reach the unlocked state. 7. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to claim 6, wherein the locking element (24) is configured as a locking button (24) arranged to be pressed into or towards the grip portion (23), and the release element (25) is configured as a release button (25) arranged to be pressed into or towards the grip portion (23). 8. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the shaft (11) comprises a bend (80) and/or a curved section. 9. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to claim 8, wherein the bend (80) and/or curved section is at the attachment end (13). 10. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the in-situ bender (10) comprises a manipulation handle (40) for manipulating the clamping mechanism (15) to selectively reach the locked state and the unlocked state. 11. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the passage (17) is sized and shaped to receive the rod sideways within the passage. 12. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the passage (17) extends cross-sectionally through the attachment end (13) of the shaft (11). 13. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the clamping mechanism (15) comprises a clamping engagement surface (37), and wherein the clamping mechanism (15) is configured to sandwich the rod (3) between the clamping engagement surface (37) and at least a bottom seat of the hook. 14. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the hook (16) forms a passage (17) for the spinal rod (3), and wherein the passage (16) defines a passage axis (PA), which is oriented non-orthogonally to the shaft axis (A1). 15. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the clamping mechanism (15) is coupled to a body (32) of the in-situ bender (10) formed by the shaft (11) and the grip portion (23) by a pin-in-slot coupling at the attachment end (13). 16. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to claim 15, wherein the pin-in-slot coupling comprises a pin or protrusion (48) and an elongated slot (38) such that the pin or protrusion (48) is configured to translate within the elongated slot (38), or the elongated slot (38) is configured to translate with respect to the pin or protrusion (48). 17. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to claim 15 or 16, wherein the clamping mechanism (15) comprises a first lever link (26) extending along the shaft (11), and wherein the pin-in-slot coupling forms a pivot point for the first lever link (26) to allow the first lever link (26) to pivot while being allowed to translate longitudinally with respect to the shaft (11 ). 18. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the rod (3) has a rod diameter RD, the clamping mechanism (15) comprises one or more lever links (26, 29) coupled at the handling end (12) to a body (32) of the in-situ bender (10) formed by the shaft (11) and the grip portion (23) by a handling end coupling (34, 43), the one or more lever links (26, 29) having in the locked state a combined length L between the handling end coupling and a clamping engagement surface (37) of the one or more lever links (26, 29), the hook (16) comprises a bottom seat (18) facing the clamping engagement surface (37), and the distance in the locked state between the bottom seat (18) and the handling end coupling is CD, and wherein in the locked state L + RD is greater than or equal to CD, and more preferably L + RD is at least 5 mm greater than CD, or more preferably L + RD is between 0.1 mm to 1 mm greater than CD. 19. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the clamping mechanism (15) comprises a first level link (26) extending along the shaft (11), and comprising an engaging surface (37) at the attachment end (13) to be pressed against the spinal rod (3) in the locked state. 20. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the clamping mechanism (15) comprises a first level link (26) comprising a first lever extension (36) extending on a first side of the grip portion (23) inside and/or outside of the grip portion (23), and a second lever extension extending on a second, different side of the grip portion (23) inside and/or outside of the grip portion (23). 21. The spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the clamping mechanism (15) comprises a first level link (26) extending along the shaft (11), and wherein the first lever link (26) is arranged at least in an elongated groove in the shaft (11). 22. A spinal in-situ bender assembly comprising the spinal in-situ bender (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, and a spinal rod (3) received sideways in the passage (17).
